project_profile.deployment_model: Python库，通过pip安装后作为PyTorch的扩展模块使用。典型部署场景为：开发者导入mx_driving模块，调用NPU加速算子进行自动驾驶模型训练或推理。库包含C/C++算子实现（通过CMake编译为.so）和Python API封装。

trust_boundaries.untrusted_side: External input data (model files, training data)
User-provided file paths (torch.load paths, data paths)

files.reason: Contains eval() expression evaluation for decorator application, potential code injection if decorator expressions are user-controlled
Monkey patching framework with dynamic module import, module names from patch definitions
Uses torch.load() without weights_only=True for loading cached test data, pickle deserialization vulnerability
Loads shared library from LD_LIBRARY_PATH via ctypes, library path can be manipulated by environment variable
Uses torch.load() without weights_only=True for loading .pt files, pickle deserialization vulnerability
Uses torch.load() and torch.save() for model loading/saving, potential deserialization vulnerability
Uses subprocess.check_call() for cmake/git commands, commands are hardcoded but git rev-parse uses cwd parameter
Uses ctypes.CDLL() to load shared library, path derived from build output
Uses subprocess.Popen() and subprocess.getstatusoutput() for CI testing, commands may be configurable
Package initialization, sets environment variable ASCEND_CUSTOM_OPP_PATH, loads shared library for chip detection
Patcher module initialization, imports torch/torch_npu and creates default patcher
Dataset loading logic, uses json.load() for agents_num file, no pickle usage
Pybind11 bindings for C++ operators, exposes C++ functions to Python
Sparse convolution functional wrappers, calls C++ bindings via mx_driving._C
Voxelization operator wrapper, basic input validation for empty tensors

entry_points.justification: eval() used for decorator expression resolution within patcher framework. Decorator expressions come from predefined patch definitions (developer-controlled), not user input. Risk is limited to developers creating custom patches with malicious decorator strings.
entry_points.description: Decorator expression evaluation in patcher framework

entry_points.justification: torch.load() used without weights_only=True to load cached test data from file paths. File paths come from test cache directory (developer-controlled environment) but could be manipulated if cache directory is writable by other users.
entry_points.description: Loading cached test data via torch.load()

entry_points.justification: torch.load() used without weights_only=True to load .pt data files from user-provided path. Path is constructor argument, attacker can provide malicious .pt file if they control data source.
entry_points.description: Loading training data from .pt files via torch.load()

entry_points.justification: torch.load() used without weights_only=True to load pretrained model weights from args.continue_path. Path is CLI argument, attacker controlling command line can provide malicious checkpoint.

Loads libdrvdsmi_host.so from LD_LIBRARY_PATH. Library search path can be manipulated by local user via environment variable, but requires the attacker to place malicious library in accessible location.
Modifies ASCEND_CUSTOM_OPP_PATH environment variable, combining with existing value. Existing env value can be set by local user before importing mx_driving.
git rev-parse executed in project root directory during build. Command is hardcoded, cwd is BASE_DIR (project root). Only executed during pip install/build, attacker cannot trigger this at runtime.

attack_surfaces: Python API Input Validation: Tensor shape/type validation in ops modules (sparse_functional.py, voxelization.py)
File Loading: torch.load() in model examples and test data cache (DriverAgent/data.py, data_cache.py)
Environment Variable: LD_LIBRARY_PATH for library loading (get_chip_info.py), ASCEND_CUSTOM_OPP_PATH (__init__.py)
Monkey Patching: Dynamic module replacement via patcher framework (patcher.py, patch.py)
Decorator Evaluation: eval() in patch.py for decorator expression resolution
C Extension Bindings: pybind.cpp exposes C++ operators to Python without validation
Model Checkpoint Loading: torch.load() in DriverAgent/train.py for pretrained weights
